M-043.NAME: mtproto-provisioning
M-043.owns: Deterministic assignment identity, CTA MTProto hostname generation, legacy u-* preservation, KPprotoN fake-TLS link generation, superseded official MTProxy per-user secret generation, tg://proxy assembly, and idempotent reissue policy.
M-043.purpose: Port the useful KPprotoN provisioning logic into KrotPN as Python/FastAPI domain code backed by Postgres assignments.

exports.fn-generate-sni: Generate a stable user-bound wildcard-safe SNI under krotpn.xyz.
exports.fn-generate-cta-sni: Generate a Phase-47 CTA-prefixed wildcard-safe SNI for newly issued assignments.
exports.fn-shorten-public-user-id: Derive a 7-hex public suffix without exposing raw numeric user IDs.
exports.fn-select-cta-prefix: Validate explicit CTA prefixes or choose a stable pseudo-random allowed prefix.
exports.fn-derive-fake-tls-secret: Build a per-SNI Telegram fake-TLS secret from configured base secret and private salt.
exports.fn-derive-official-mtproxy-secret: Build a 16-byte official MTProxy secret for one canonical user assignment.
exports.fn-issue-user-proxy: Create or reuse a user's canonical MTProto assignment and return a safe response payload.
exports.fn-build-tg-link: Assemble tg://proxy from server, port, and derived secret for the authenticated user.
exports.fn-build-secure-official-tg-link: Assemble tg://proxy with secret=dd<32-hex-secret> for the authenticated owner after Phase-40 execution.

source-transfer.reuse: Stable SNI derivation, per-SNI derived credentials, supersession warning semantics.
source-transfer.rewrite: Implement in Python with deterministic test vectors and KrotPN settings.

constraints.constraint-1: Knowing base domain, port, and base secret must not be enough to authenticate without issued derived credential.
constraints.constraint-2: Base secret or salt rotation invalidates old links and must require explicit reissue messaging.
constraints.constraint-3: Provisioning must be idempotent for repeated verified-registration retries.
constraints.constraint-4: Missing or blank MTProto secret configuration must fail proxy issuance safely without crashing unrelated backend startup.
constraints.constraint-5: Phase-40 official MTProxy links must treat fake-TLS SNI-derived links as legacy and must reissue explicitly rather than silently claiming compatibility.
constraints.constraint-6: Phase-41 fake-TLS links must treat official dd-secret assignments as superseded and must reissue users explicitly rather than silently claiming compatibility.
constraints.constraint-7: Phase-47 new assignments use CTA-prefixed one-label hostnames, but existing active u-* assignments must be preserved without silent mass reissue.

phase-41-plan.step-1: Restore owner payloads to server=u-*.krotpn.xyz and secret=ee... fake-TLS format.
phase-41-plan.step-2: Detect official credential-mode assignments and return reissue-required state before showing a stale dd link as active.
phase-41-plan.step-3: Keep SNI, base secret, salt, and derived secret redacted in logs and admin surfaces.

evidence.evidence-1: 2026-05-14: Phase-29 target pytest passed for deterministic SNI, KPprotoN fake-TLS vector, verified-user guard, idempotency, reissue, and config failure paths.
evidence.evidence-2: 2026-05-17: Clean VPS hotfix added regression coverage for blank MTProto env values being normalized to missing config and rejected at issuance time.
evidence.evidence-3: 2026-05-19: Phase-40 plan adds official MTProxy per-user secret issuance because the fake-TLS data-plane did not pass real req_pq availability.
evidence.evidence-4: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 verification design supersedes official dd-secret issuance for production and returns owner payloads to the KPprotoN fake-TLS SNI model.
evidence.evidence-5: 2026-05-19: Phase-41 local tests passed for derived_per_sni defaults, ee fake-TLS owner payloads, explicit official-mode reissue, owner API behavior, and Phase-34 verified-registration integration.
evidence.evidence-6: 2026-06-01: Phase-47 local tests passed 14/14 for CTA allow-list, public short IDs, explicit prefix validation, one-label SNI, final-SNI fake-TLS binding, and legacy u-* assignment preservation.

M-043.hotspot: H-029: Changing MTProto base secret or salt invalidates issued Telegram proxy links unless reissue is explicit
